title: Intensely Caramelized Japanese Oysters Noodles
description: A funky and shattering Japanese dish from Shikoku featuring caramelized oysters, perfect for a hangover cure, and vegetarian friendly by substituting traditional ingredients with vegetarian alternatives.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the noodles according to the package instructions.
In a large skillet, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat and add the oyster mushrooms, cooking until they release their liquid and start to brown.
Add the soy sauce, sake, mirin, and sugar to the skillet, stirring to combine, and cook for 2-3 minutes or until the sauce is caramelized and the mushrooms are coated.
Drain the noodles and add them to the skillet, tossing to combine with the mushrooms and sauce.
Transfer the noodles to a serving dish and top with green onions, sesame seeds, and grated daikon if using.
To enhance the caramelization, broil the noodles in the oven for an additional 2-3 minutes, watching carefully to prevent burning.
Garnish with additional green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
